data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: YouTube uses a microservices architecture to scale and manage different components like video upload, streaming, recommendation, and ads independently.
data.technical_spec: Distributed microservices over a cloud platform such as Google Cloud, utilizing containerization with Kubernetes.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: A global CDN is used to deliver video content quickly and reliably to users around the world.
data.technical_spec: Use of global PoPs (Points of Presence) with caching servers distributed worldwide.

data.title: Data Analytics Infrastructure
data.description: Infrastructure to process, analyze, and store vast amounts of data for recommendations and insights.
data.technical_spec: Hadoop, BigQuery, and other big data technologies.

data.title: Adaptive Bitrate Streaming
data.description: Technology allowing video quality to adjust in real-time to the user's internet speed.
data.technical_spec: HLS (HTTP Live Streaming) or DASH (Dynamic Adaptive Streaming over HTTP).

data.title: API Gateway
data.description: Centralized entry point for internal and external API requests.
data.technical_spec: Use of RESTful APIs with OAuth 2.0 for authentication.
